meta.tagline: A K2061/K2088-class VAST engine bracketed by a constant Summit voice
meta.nextStep: Moog as the FIRST production Cmajor filter block (ADR-0012 migration step, avoids the double-build; fade engine is latent/live now that v5.1 shipped) → v6 dynamic graph

items.title: v1 — Skeleton end-to-end
items.summary: 1 osc, 2-slot DSP chain, ADSR, 8-voice, plain UI.

items.title: v2 — Layer abstraction
items.summary: Voice split into per-note runtime + Layer owning DSP + ParamSnapshot.

items.title: v3 — Algorithm abstraction
items.summary: Selectable algorithm = ordered walk through a per-Layer block palette.

items.title: v4 — Multi-Layer Programs
items.summary: Program holds 2 Layers; shared voice pool; Layer/Split/Dual routing.

items.title: v4.5 — K2061 re-positioning + Summit UI foundation
items.summary: Engine re-positioned to K2061/K2088 VAST + constant Summit spine; UI foundation. KB OCR fix outstanding.

children.title: Pirkle synth-book OCR fix in k2000-kb
children.summary: PDF text layer is garbled mojibake; needs an OCR fallback in kb-build.

items.title: v5 — The Constant Summit Spine
items.summary: Always-present selectable filter-model library + Huggett flagship; live click-free hot-swap; stereo, per-Layer.

children.title: v5.0 — Nonlinear Huggett (plain-tanh spine)
children.summary: Three asymmetric tanh stages + clean HP pre-filter. Remediated post-UAT (ADAA dropped).

children.title: v5 — Hearing-safety output limiter (default-ON)
children.summary: SAFETY: a global output/amp brickwall limiter that hard-caps the signal to a safe ceiling so extreme resonance/separation/self-oscillation can never blow the listener's ears or speakers. ON by default; defeatable ONLY via an explicit operator action gated behind a hearing-safety warning (never silently off, never off by preset). Scheduled immediately AFTER the Cmajor spike — user priority.

children.title: Fix Separation click + gradual drive onset
children.summary: SHIPPED: two spine continuity-at-0 fixes. (1) Separation click — 12 dB LP/HP no longer switch single<->parallel-sum at sep=0 (6 dB jump); always half-gain parallel, so sep=0 = one LP and it widens continuously. (2) Pre/post drive onset — the shaper engaged a fixed bias + makeup gain the instant drive>0; now blends in by the drive amount (identity at 0, gradual). Tests: HuggettSeparationClickTests, HuggettDriveOnsetTests.

children.title: v5.1 — Q17 click-free model hot-swap
children.summary: SHIPPED: per-voice equal-power crossfade (~25 ms default, 2–100 ms via spine.modelFadeMs), coalesce depth-1; per-voice state heap→in-place dual-buffer (kMaxSpineStateBytes=512, static_assert governed); Q17b closed by note-start snap-bind. Production library still has ONE model (Huggett) — fade engine is latent until Moog lands. 179 tests, 0 failed.

children.title: v5.2 — Real Separation / dual-filter
children.summary: Two independently-tuned 2-pole sections; proper Separation; Summit dual routings; works in 12 and 24 dB.

children.title: v5.3 — HQ oversampling tiers + on-screen keyboard
children.summary: Light/Normal/Heavy/Full OS with independent live/render selectors; playable + MIDI-display keyboard.

children.title: v5.4 — Moog ladder (2nd filter model)
children.summary: 4-pole self-oscillating; pole-mix BP/HP; bassComp; DC blocker. Gated on hot-swap + OS tiers.

children.title: v5.5 — Oberheim SEM (3rd filter model)
children.summary: 2-pole multimode SVF, non-self-oscillating; LP→notch→HP morph + BP.

children.title: v5.6 — Yamaha CS-80 (4th filter model)
children.summary: Dual-channel voice (I+II), each a resonant 2-pole HP + 2-pole LP in series; self-oscillating. Signature 'sine bass' = a self-oscillating filter tuned low used as a clean sine sub (a patch technique, not a dedicated circuit) — capture the blooming/gliding self-osc character (vs the Moog's clean played-note sub-osc, which is the controllable alternative shipped first). Verify topology against the CS-80 service manual before implementing. Append-only library entry.

children.title: v5 — External VST test harness
children.summary: Tooling to load + measure external reference VSTs (e.g. Arturia Mini V) and capture golden response data — magnitude/phase grids, self-osc pitch tracking, driven harmonic snapshots — for filter-model validation and A/B. Directly unblocks the Moog Arturia-match calibration gate (2026-06-22-moog-cmajor-dsp-design.md, Spec 1) and any future model-vs-reference comparison.

children.title: v5 — Filter-validation standard: 7000-pt log dual-sweep
children.summary: STANDARD (set 2026-06-23): every spine filter validated by a 7000-point LOG sweep 10 Hz–25 kHz, in BOTH forms — (1) frequency-response magnitude |H(f)| (rolloff + resonant peak; the Arturia-comparison format) and (2) self-osc pitch-tracking (±3% gate only ≤~4 kHz, measure-report above due to base-rate delay drift). Log-spaced, in a SEPARATE opt-in test target (too slow for every build; fast suite keeps a ~7-point subset). Applies to all models. Deliverable: the reusable dual-sweep harness + BACK-TEST Huggett against it.

items.title: v6 — Dynamic VAST Graph Routing
items.summary: Wired serial+parallel per-voice DSP graph; splits/joins; visual wiring editor.

children.title: First move towards a proper GUI based on sketches

items.title: v9 — Multipart, Cascade & Multis
items.summary: A/B/Split/Layer surface; toward 32 layers / 256 voices; Multis (≤16).

items.summary: Sample/keymap sources (the big v11+ piece, decomposed out).

items.title: v15 — Filter Model Expansion
items.summary: Diode ladder + more topologies; gates re-anchored to hardware.

items.title: v16 — Modulation Deepening
items.summary: Expanded mod matrix, more LFO/env, advanced voice modes.

items.title: v17 — Performance Layer
items.summary: Arpeggiator, sequencer, macros/controllers.

items.summary: Per-note pitch/pressure/slide.

items.summary: Full target GUI, theming, hi-DPI/scalable UI.

items.title: v21 — Preset Ecosystem & Factory Library
items.summary: Packs, sharing, curated factory sounds.

items.title: v22 — Performance & Scale Hardening
items.summary: 256-voice everywhere, SIMD, multicore.

items.title: v23 — Platform Expansion
items.summary: AU/AUv3, CLAP, more hosts/formats.

items.title: v24 — AI-Assisted Sound Design
items.summary: Local-LLM patch generation/morphing.

items.title: v25 — Bernie 1.0: Commercial Flagship Release
items.summary: Everything converges; 1.0 ship.

items.title: Cmajor Spike — decision gate (pre-v6)
items.summary: DONE (Spike I + II) → ADR-0012 (Proposed): GO / hybrid. Equivalence bit-exact (linear + nonlinear); data boundary solved (value endpoint runtime-settable; external float[] is codegen-baked-only); clean FilterModel slot-in, RT-safe; AOT generated-C++ committed (no engine embed, no CI dependency). Perf (equal-work): single node ~1.95×, but a FUSED per-voice processor (SVF+sat+drive in one advance) ~1.3× — author v6 as fused processors, not micro-nodes. Boundary: Cmajor below the per-voice DSP-block line, JUCE everywhere else. RATIFIED — ADR-0012 Accepted (both spikes complete).

children.title: Toolchain + spike harness
children.summary: Stand up Cmajor in an isolated spike harness OUTSIDE the VST3 target: choose AOT C++ codegen (cmaj generate --target=cpp) vs the runtime/JIT lib, and confirm Cmajor's license is compatible (note it in the ADR). Gate: a trivial gain patch compiles and processes a buffer with the expected gain.

children.title: Port the linear TPT SVF
children.summary: Reimplement NlSvfCell's LINEAR TPT core (cutoff/resonance, LP/BP/HP taps) as a Cmajor processor — the bounded pilot surface. Deliverable: an SVF Cmajor patch driven by cutoff/res. (Nonlinear res-sat is out of scope for the pilot.)

children.title: Numeric equivalence gate
children.summary: Compare the Cmajor SVF against the C++ NlSvfCell across cutoff/resonance sweeps using the existing tests/testdsp magnitude-response + NSR helpers. Gate: responses match within a tolerance (assert; CI-fail on regression).

children.title: JUCE/APVTS integration
children.summary: Host the Cmajor SVF inside a JUCE AudioProcessor path: block processing + parameter passing (cutoff/res from atomics, the snapshot pattern) + RT-safety (no audio-thread alloc). Gate: audio flows through the JUCE-hosted Cmajor node with correct params.

children.title: 256-voice perf model (the crux)
children.summary: Benchmark the per-voice cost: 256 Cmajor instances vs one patch with internal polyphony. Measure CPU + memory at 256 voices against the current C++ spine baseline. Gate: numbers vs C++ baseline that fit (or explicitly fail) the per-voice budget — this decides go/no-go.

children.title: Windows CI feasibility
children.summary: Decided (ADR-0012): commit the generated C++ — no Cmajor toolchain in CI. Spike code (generated C++ + adapters + tests) lives in the test target Windows CI builds; PENDING confirmation green on the next CI run.

children.title: ADR-0012 + recommendation
children.summary: Synthesize findings (equivalence, 256-voice perf, integration friction, licensing, dev experience) into ADR-0012. Outcomes are not just go/no-go: a HYBRID (Cmajor for some/all DSP, JUCE for host/UI/params) is a valid recommendation. If adopting, define the JUCE↔Cmajor boundary, the migration slot/approach, and whether v6's graph is authored in Cmajor.

items.title: Cmajor Adoption — hybrid (coexist with JUCE; below the per-voice DSP-block line)
items.summary: ADR-0012 (Accepted) — GO/hybrid: Cmajor owns per-voice DSP-block math (AOT generated-C++ behind thin C++ adapters), JUCE keeps host/UI/params/MIDI + the C++ voice pool; v6's graph authored as FUSED per-voice Cmajor processors (no double-build). Both spikes done: equivalence bit-exact, data boundary solved, realistic perf ~1.3× (budget into the 256-voice perf gate). Sign-off done. First step: port a real shipping DSP block behind its existing interface; move adapter state heap→in-place (Q17/Q18); measure oversampled-nonlinear cost at v5.3.

continuousThreads.title: DSP test-harness coverage
continuousThreads.summary: Grows to cover every spine/source/FX component; gates releases.

continuousThreads.title: Per-voice perf gate
continuousThreads.summary: 256 voices × full stereo × graph DSP; profiling as a release gate.

continuousThreads.title: Incremental GUI toward the target aesthetic
continuousThreads.summary: Each phase advances the visual design; no deferred 'real GUI'.

continuousThreads.title: Security-scan CI/CD gate
continuousThreads.summary: SAST + SCA (cppcheck/clang-tidy/CodeQL + dep/CVE scan) wired as a CI/CD GATE (fails the build on regression), not just a baseline. Package the setup as a reusable skill if it generalizes. Scheduled after v5.2 + the Cmajor spike.
